body:
@sepo-agent /answer 

- For example, if we tag the agent for reviewing in the chat, we should display as labels 
- the same should be true for orchestrator; but we should not remove the orchestrator tag until it finishes 

Can you plan for it?

comments.body:
I checked issue #25 and related context. There’s no parent/tracker linked from #25, but related issue [#19](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/19) exists and its implementation PR [#20](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/20) is still open (not merged), so `main` does not yet include explicit `/orchestrate` routing.

Current behavior in `main`:
- Mention runs are routed in [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml) and only apply the fixed `agent` status label via [`add-label.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/add-label.ts).
- Label-triggered runs are handled by [`agent-label.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-label.yml), which removes the triggering `agent/*` label after router success.
- Orchestrator handoff is dispatched from [`dispatch-agent-orchestrator.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/dispatch-agent-orchestrator.ts) into [`agent-orchestrator.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml), with no dedicated orchestrator activity label lifecycle.

Proposed implementation approach:
1. Add route-activity label management (separate from fixed `agent` status label), with explicit add/remove operations in CLI helpers.
2. Apply corresponding activity label when a route starts (review, fix-pr, implement, orchestrator path).
3. Remove activity labels in terminal workflow steps (`if: always()`), so cleanup happens even on failures.
4. Special-case orchestrator: keep its label until orchestrator workflow actually completes; do not let generic trigger-label cleanup remove it early.
5. Keep trigger-label semantics (`agent/*`) and status/activity semantics separate to avoid accidental retriggers and cleanup races.

Execution plan (for approval):
1. Extend GitHub label helpers in [`github.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/github.ts) with remove-label support.
2. Add a new CLI for route activity labels (add/remove by route + target kind/number), and tests under [`.agent/src/__tests__`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/tree/main/.agent/src/__tests__).
3. Wire label-add at route start in [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml) and workflow entry points where needed.
4. Wire label-remove at route completion in [`agent-review.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-review.yml), [`agent-fix-pr.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-fix-pr.yml), [`agent-implement.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml), and [`agent-orchestrator.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml).
5. Update [`agent-label.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-label.yml) cleanup logic so orchestrator-tag cleanup is deferred until orchestrator completion.
6. Update assertions in [`envelope.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts) and docs in [`supported-workflows.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md).

Please confirm and I’ll start implementation.

Clarifications to unblock cleanly:
1. Should “corresponding label” reuse trigger names (`agent/review`, `agent/orchestrate`) or use separate non-trigger activity labels (recommended)?
2. For orchestrator, should label removal happen when `agent-orchestrator.yml` ends, or only after the full multi-step automation chain reaches stop?

title: Display the corresponding label when running the action
